steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence within the passage as a whole. Map how the underlined sentence relates to both the sentence before it and the sentence after it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It offers details to clarify the claim made in the previous sentence': The previous sentence claims community science is “effective and engaging.” The underlined sentence lists three specific benefits (more data, scientific insight, youth interest) that explain why — directly clarifying that claim. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It establishes the limits of the conclusion presented in the sentence that follows': The sentence that follows describes the Merrill butterfly study. The underlined sentence does not restrict or qualify that study in any way. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It provides a contrast with an idea presented in the previous sentence': The underlined sentence agrees with and supports the previous sentence; it does not introduce any contrasting idea. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It identifies the purpose of the study described in the sentence that follows': The sentence that follows describes the butterfly study's topic and scope. The underlined sentence mentions general benefits of community science, not the purpose of that specific study. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
